meta.description: Google Chrome browser. Multi-tab, CDP-based automation via BrowserWin.exe. Supports page navigation, DOM interaction, clipboard, screenshots, and JS execution.

Chrome is automated via BrowserWin.exe using the Chrome DevTools Protocol (CDP). There are NO UIA AutomationIds for browser content — all element targeting uses CSS selectors, XPath, or semantic role queries via CDP / accessibility tree.
Unified commands (shared with KeyWin; routed by helper context): LISTWINDOWS target="chrome" → list tabs/instances (BrowserWin handles) LISTWINDOWS target="SYSTEM" → list all — returns mix of HWND and PAGE entries LAUNCH target="chrome" → launch or attach; returns tab list READ parameter="" → body.innerText (full page text) READ parameter="css:#id" → element text or input.value SENDKEYS parameter="css:#inp|text"→ focus element by selector, then type CLICKID parameter="css:#btn" → click by CSS selector CLICKID parameter="role:button[name]" → click by ARIA role
CDP protocol commands (BrowserWin.exe only — prefix CDP_): CDP_NAVIGATE parameter="https://..."→ navigate tab (fast, direct; keyboard alt = {CTRL+L}+url+{ENTER}) CDP_NEWPAGE parameter="" → open blank tab; returns PAGE:id (keyboard alt = SENDKEYS {CTRL+T}) CDP_CLOSEPAGE target="PAGE:id" → close tab (keyboard alt = SENDKEYS {CTRL+W}) CDP_EXECUTE parameter="<js>" → Runtime.evaluate; returns serialized result CDP_PAGESOURCE parameter="" → accessibility tree snapshot (Page.getAccessibilityTree) CDP_SCREENSHOT parameter="" → base64 PNG of full tab (Page.captureScreenshot)
Selector syntax for parameter: "css:<sel>" CSS selector e.g. "css:#username" "css:<sel>|<text>" focus by sel, type (used with SENDKEYS) "xpath:<expr>" XPath expression "role:<role>[name]" ARIA role + name e.g. "role:button[Search]" "" page-level (for READ/SENDKEYS without element target)
KILL routing: KILL on PAGE:<id> or chrome:xxx → BrowserWin.exe (CDP tab/process close) KILL on HANDLE:<hwnd> → KeyWin.exe (OS process termination) The HelperRegistry resolves the correct helper by target prefix.
Keyboard shortcuts (via SENDKEYS — no element target, window-level): {CTRL+T} new tab {CTRL+W} close tab {CTRL+L} focus address bar {CTRL+R} reload {F5} reload {ESCAPE} cancel {TAB} next field {ENTER} submit {F12} DevTools {CTRL+F} find in page {ALT+LEFT} back {ALT+RIGHT} forward

Group.label: Browser process
Group.note: Process-level operations — session discovery, launch, CDP port management.

Control.label: The Chrome browser process. Target: "chrome".

LISTWINDOWS target="chrome" → list open tabs (BrowserWin routes to CDP port discovery). LAUNCH opens Chrome if not running, or attaches to existing instance. CDP_NEWPAGE → open a blank tab (preferred over SENDKEYS {CTRL+T} for reliable PAGE:id return). KILL target="chrome" → terminates the Chrome process via BrowserWin.exe.

action.note: list all sessions — BrowserWin returns CDP tab list, KeyWin returns HWND list
launch or attach; returns tab list
open blank tab via CDP; returns PAGE:id (keyboard alt: SENDKEYS {CTRL+T})
open tab at URL directly via CDP

Group.label: Per-tab content operations
Group.note: All content commands target PAGE:<id>. Universal primitives + CDP_ protocol commands.

Control.label: A single Chrome tab. Target: PAGE:<tabId>.

Universal commands (READ, SENDKEYS, CLICKID) work the same as on UIA targets. CDP_ commands are direct DevTools Protocol calls — fast but BrowserWin-only. CDP_NAVIGATE is preferred over the keyboard scenario ({CTRL+L}+url+{ENTER}) for speed and reliable load-complete signalling. CDP_PAGESOURCE returns the accessibility tree (not raw HTML — use CDP_EXECUTE document.documentElement.outerHTML for raw HTML).

action.note: navigate tab; waits for load (keyboard alt: {CTRL+L}+url+{ENTER})
close this tab via CDP (keyboard alt: SENDKEYS {CTRL+W})

Group.label: Semantic page-element patterns
Group.note: Not real AutomationIds — reusable CSS selector patterns for common page structures.

Control.note: Chrome address / omnibox bar — not in DOM; use SENDKEYS {CTRL+L}
Control.label: Chrome address bar (omnibar).
Not a DOM element. Focus with {CTRL+L} via SENDKEYS on the tab, then type URL and press {ENTER} to navigate. Prefer CDP_NAVIGATE on the tab target for reliable load-complete signalling.
Generic text input field on a web page.

Target: use specific CSS selector or id for the actual input. SENDKEYS with "css:#fieldId|text" focuses and types. Use CLICKID first if the field needs a click to activate (e.g. custom dropdowns). Check with EXECUTE: document.querySelector('#id').value to verify.

action.note: set value via JS (no events fired)

Control.label: Form submit button.
Most forms: CLICKID "css:button[type=submit]" or SENDKEYS {ENTER} on focused input. Verify submission by READ or checking URL change via EXECUTE: window.location.href.
The page body — all visible text content.

READ with no parameter returns body.innerText (all visible text). For specific sections use READ with css: selector. For structured data use EXECUTE: JSON.stringify(…) to return arrays/objects. innerText includes only visible text; use innerHTML or textContent via EXECUTE for more.
